description: Frozen thesis runtime execution registry compiled from study workbook instance thesis_experiment_program_revised_v1.xlsx.

experiments.title: Class weighting experiment
experiments.notes: Operationally narrowed weighting lock uses ridge as provisional model for executability; if E06 later locks a different winner, rerun E07 with that frozen model before final scientific lock interpretation.

experiments.title: Hyperparameter strategy experiment
experiments.notes: Operationally narrowed tuning lock uses ridge with class_weight_policy=none as provisional defaults; if E06 or E07 later lock different winners, rerun E08 under the frozen upstream model/weighting choices.

experiments.title: Whole-brain versus ROI experiment
experiments.notes: Atlas-derived sensory ROI voxel comparison between whole_brain_masked and roi_masked_predefined. ROI masked extraction is restricted to atlas ROI union intersected with common valid sample support. The unresolved 29 spherical ROI component remains intentionally excluded. E09 remains advisory/supporting only and is not a frozen lock dependency.

experiments.title: Interpretability stability robustness support
experiments.notes: Derived robustness support from E16 anchors only; model-behavior evidence only.

experiments.title: Final within-person confirmatory analysis (ROI masked)
experiments.notes: ROI-only companion to E16; preserves official whole-brain E16 definitions.

experiments.title: Final cross-person transfer analysis (ROI masked)
experiments.notes: ROI-only companion to E17; preserves official whole-brain E17 definitions.

search_spaces.notes: Workbook-executable target grid for E01 target-lock comparison.

study_designs.question: Which target, split, model, weighting, and tuning choices should be locked before confirmatory analysis?
study_designs.generalization_claim: Supports only method-choice decisions for the current dataset.

constraints.reason: Cross-person transfer requires ordered subject pairs, not a single-subject slice.
Do not interpret task-specific runs without sufficient per-class counts.
Do not interpret modality-specific runs without sufficient per-class counts.

study_designs.question: Does the locked pipeline generalize to unseen sessions within each participant?
study_designs.stopping_rule: Stop only after all subjects are run.
study_designs.notes: Primary Chapter 4 result; linked to E16 plus E12-E14.

study_designs.question: Does the frozen mapping transfer across participants under directional train/test separation?
study_designs.generalization_claim: Directional cross-person transfer only; not population inference.
study_designs.notes: Primary Chapter 4 transfer result; linked to E17.

study_designs.question: Do the main conclusions survive confound diagnostics, temporal separation, task/modality holdout, and session-influence checks?
study_designs.generalization_claim: Supports interpretation and limitation boundaries, not the core claim by itself.
study_designs.stopping_rule: Complete all planned robustness checks or document omissions.

constraints.reason: Session-identity diagnostics test confounds; they cannot become confirmatory target claims.
Temporal drift analyses require explicit temporal separation, not random record splits.

study_designs.question: Can the frozen confirmatory package be rerun deterministically with matching artifacts and summaries?
study_designs.generalization_claim: Operational reproducibility of the locked pipeline.
study_designs.stopping_rule: Stop after two clean reruns are compared.

constraints.reason: Rerun audit must use the same frozen configuration across both reruns.

study_rigor_checklists.fairness_or_applicability_notes: Method-lock work should not be presented as final performance evidence.
Within-person results remain participant-specific and split-specific.
Cross-person transfer is a domain-shift test, not a population claim.
Robustness checks challenge interpretation rather than replace primary evidence.
Applies to reproducibility and software auditability rather than subgroup fairness.
study_rigor_checklists.analysis_notes: Compare artifacts, summaries, and frozen configs.

analysis_plans.primary_contrast: Compare decision options within the same split/subject context.
analysis_plans.secondary_contrasts: Target vs split vs model vs weighting vs tuning sensitivity summaries.
analysis_plans.interpretation_rules: Use only to lock decisions, not to overclaim performance.

analysis_plans.primary_contrast: Locked model vs held-out-session performance by subject.
analysis_plans.secondary_contrasts: Permutation support, trivial baseline support, explanation stability.
analysis_plans.interpretation_rules: Interpret within-person only and under exact split logic.

analysis_plans.primary_contrast: A->B and B->A frozen transfer comparison.
analysis_plans.interpretation_rules: Do not reframe as a population-level result.

analysis_plans.primary_contrast: Temporal, task, modality, confound, and jackknife stress tests vs locked baseline.
analysis_plans.secondary_contrasts: Sensitivity tables and narrative limitation statements.
analysis_plans.interpretation_rules: Use to strengthen validity boundaries and discussion, not to cherry-pick better scores.
analysis_plans.notes: Supports Chapter 4 robustness and Chapter 5 limitations.

analysis_plans.primary_contrast: Frozen rerun 1 vs frozen rerun 2 output agreement.
analysis_plans.secondary_contrasts: Config hash, artifact hash, and summary-value agreement.
